questions.q: In which city was Rama crowned king?
questions.explanation: Rama was crowned king of Ayodhya, the capital of the kingdom of Kosala.

questions.q: What major event in Rama's life had to be completed before his coronation could take place?
options: His fourteen-year exile in the forest
The building of the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Rama returned to Ayodhya and was crowned only after his fourteen-year exile in the forest had been fulfilled.

questions.q: Who became queen alongside Rama at his coronation?
questions.explanation: Sita, Rama's faithful wife, was crowned beside him as his queen.

questions.q: Which loyal brother had ruled Ayodhya as a regent and returned the kingdom to Rama?
questions.explanation: Bharata had ruled as regent in Rama's name, placing Rama's sandals on the throne, and gladly returned the kingdom upon Rama's return.

questions.q: The reign that began with Rama's coronation is remembered as what?
questions.explanation: Rama's reign, known as Rama Rajya, is remembered as a golden age of justice and prosperity.

questions.q: What was the general mood in Ayodhya during Rama's coronation?
questions.explanation: The card describes Rama being crowned amid universal rejoicing throughout the kingdom.

questions.q: Which devoted ally and brother of Rama was present at the coronation, having stood by him throughout the exile?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana accompanied Rama through the entire exile and stood beside him at the coronation.

questions.q: From which great war did Rama return victorious shortly before his coronation?
options: The war against Ravana of Lanka
questions.explanation: Rama returned to Ayodhya after defeating the demon king Ravana of Lanka.

questions.q: Whose throne did Rama finally ascend at his coronation?
options: The throne of Ayodhya (the Ikshvaku throne)
questions.explanation: Rama ascended the ancestral throne of Ayodhya, the seat of the Ikshvaku dynasty.

questions.q: What type of subject does the 'Coronation of Rama' card represent in the deck?
questions.explanation: The Coronation of Rama is classified as an event in the deck.

questions.q: The Sanskrit term for a royal consecration or coronation, used for Rama's crowning, is which of the following?
questions.explanation: The coronation ceremony is called Rajyabhisheka or abhisheka, the sacred consecration of a king.

questions.q: Which sage and royal preceptor of the Ikshvaku dynasty presided over Rama's consecration?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, the family priest and guru of the Ikshvaku line, presided over Rama's coronation.

questions.q: Which devotee of Rama is traditionally honored at the coronation and rewarded by Sita with a pearl necklace?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, Rama's greatest devotee, is honored at the coronation and famously given a pearl necklace by Sita.

questions.q: Which of Rama's mothers is his birth mother, present at the coronation in Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Kausalya was Rama's birth mother and chief queen of Dasharatha, present to see her son crowned.

questions.q: Which ally, whom Rama had installed as king of Lanka, was among the guests at the coronation?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother whom Rama crowned king of Lanka, attended the coronation.

questions.q: Which monkey (vanara) king, Rama's ally from Kishkindha, came to witness the coronation?
questions.explanation: Sugriva, the vanara king of Kishkindha and Rama's ally, was present at the coronation.

questions.q: By what flying vehicle, taken from Lanka, did Rama travel back to Ayodhya for his coronation?
questions.explanation: Rama returned to Ayodhya aboard the Pushpaka Vimana, the flying chariot won from Ravana.

questions.q: On which sacred river that flows past Ayodhya was the city of the coronation situated?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya, where Rama was crowned, stands on the banks of the river Sarayu.

questions.q: When Rama first offered the kingdom to Bharata before the coronation, how did Bharata respond?
options: He demanded the throne for himself
He refused, insisting Rama be king
He divided the kingdom in two
questions.explanation: Bharata refused the throne, having only safeguarded it for Rama, and insisted that Rama be crowned.

questions.q: Which father of Rama, the previous king of Ayodhya, had died during the exile and so was absent at the coronation?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha died of grief after Rama's departure into exile, so he did not live to see the coronation.

questions.q: Before entering Ayodhya, Rama sent a messenger ahead to inform Bharata of his return. Who was that messenger?
questions.explanation: Rama sent Hanuman ahead to Bharata to announce his return and gauge Bharata's intentions before entering Ayodhya.

questions.q: The dynasty to which Rama belonged, restored to glory at his coronation, is known as which line?
questions.explanation: Rama belonged to the Solar dynasty (Suryavamsha) of the Ikshvaku kings of Ayodhya.

questions.q: In Valmiki's Ramayana, the coronation forms part of which final book (kanda) of the epic?
questions.explanation: Rama's return and coronation are narrated at the close of the Yuddha Kanda, the sixth book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: On the journey home, Rama stopped to gain the consent of which sage before reaching Ayodhya, at whose hermitage he had earlier stayed?
questions.explanation: Rama halted at the hermitage of sage Bharadwaja near Prayaga on his way back, before sending Hanuman ahead to Ayodhya.

questions.q: While Bharata governed in Rama's absence, where did he reside, refusing to live in the royal palace?
options: On the banks of the Sarayu
questions.explanation: Bharata lived ascetically at Nandigrama, ruling from there with Rama's sandals enshrined on the throne.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, water for Rama's consecration was brought from the four oceans and sacred rivers. Who poured the consecrating waters as the chief officiant?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha, together with the assembled priests, performed the abhisheka, anointing Rama with the sacred waters.

questions.q: In the Valmiki tradition, how long is Rama's righteous reign said to have lasted?
options: About one thousand years (eleven thousand years)
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana describes Rama ruling for many thousands of years (the Phala Shruti gives eleven thousand years) of ideal, prosperous reign.

questions.q: After the coronation, the assembled vanaras, bears, and rakshasas were honored before departing. Who provided them homes and hospitality during their stay in Ayodhya?
options: Rama, who hosted and richly rewarded them
questions.explanation: Rama lavishly hosted, honored, and rewarded his allies before bidding them an affectionate farewell to their homelands.

questions.q: Which of these is NOT traditionally counted among Rama's three brothers present in Ayodhya around the time of his coronation?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana was Ravana's brother and an ally, not a brother of Rama; Rama's brothers were Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: In popular tradition, the festival of Diwali is associated with which moment connected to the coronation story?
options: Rama's return to Ayodhya after exile
questions.explanation: Diwali celebrates Rama's triumphant return to Ayodhya after exile, the city lit with lamps to welcome him before his coronation.
